document.source_title: Netanyahu speaks out after video shows minister taunting activists
document.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu criticized his security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, after a video surfaced showing Ben-Gvir taunting detained activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla. The footage, which has drawn widespread condemnation, depicts Ben-Gvir berating the activists while waving an Israeli flag. Netanyahu stated that while Israel has the right to prevent flotillas supporting Hamas from entering its waters, Ben-Gvir's actions were not representative of Israel's values. The incident has sparked backlash from various political figures and international leaders.
key_takeaways: Netanyahu condemns Ben-Gvir's behavior towards detained activists.
Ben-Gvir's video shows him taunting activists with hands tied.
Netanyahu asserts Israel's right to stop flotillas supporting Hamas.
Criticism of Ben-Gvir comes from within the Israeli government.
Australia's Foreign Minister calls the footage shocking.
Activists demand more action against Israel's treatment of detainees.
document.why_it_matters: This incident highlights the tensions within the Israeli government regarding the treatment of activists and the broader implications for Israel's international relations, particularly concerning its actions in Gaza and the perception of its values on the global stage.

claims.claim_text: Netanyahu stated that Ben-Gvir's actions were not in line with Israel’s values.
claims.evidence_text: Netanyahu stressed that the manner in which Ben-Gvir had dealt with the activists was 'not in line with Israel’s values and norms.'

claims.claim_text: Ben-Gvir's video has received widespread criticism.
claims.evidence_text: The footage has received widespread criticism since its release, including from some senior figures inside the Israeli government.

entities.name: Benjamin Netanyahu
entities.description: Israeli Prime Minister who criticized the actions of his security minister.
entities.evidence_text: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has hit out at his security minister after video footage showed him taunting detained activists.

entities.name: Itamar Ben-Gvir
entities.description: Israeli National Security Minister who was filmed taunting detained activists.
entities.evidence_text: Footage released by Israel's National Security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir earlier this week had shown him berating detainees.

entities.name: Global Sumud Flotilla
entities.description: Group attempting to transport aid to Gaza, whose activists were detained.
entities.evidence_text: detained activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla who had been attempting to transport aid to Gaza.

entities.name: Gideon Sa'ar
entities.description: Israeli Foreign Minister who criticized Ben-Gvir's actions.
entities.evidence_text: Writing on X, Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar told Ben-Gvir he had 'deliberately caused damage to the state in this disgraceful performance.'

entities.name: Penny Wong
entities.description: Australia's Foreign Minister who condemned the footage.
entities.evidence_text: Australia's Foreign Minister Penny Wong called the footage 'shocking and unacceptable.'

entities.name: Rania Batrice
entities.description: Spokesperson for the flotilla who commented on the video.
entities.evidence_text: Rania Batrice, a spokesperson for the flotilla, said Ben-Gvir's video showed the world has not held Israel to account.

events.evidence_text: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has hit out at his security minister after video footage showed him taunting detained activists.

citations.title: Netanyahu speaks out after video shows minister taunting activists

source_credibility.reason: Euronews is a reputable news source providing coverage of international events.
